clean_story:
Now I will teach you Glasses.

Take two thin, clear rocks—smooth like still water frozen—but bend light like ripples in a pond. Shape them to sit before your eyes, tied tight with hide or vine, so far things become close, or blurry things sharp. They do not change the world, only how your eyes catch it, like squinting but without the ache. If fire-smoke stings your eyes, these shield them too. Hold them to your face; if all looks wrong, they are not for you. Glasses.
